source: Before our next meet, I plan my study blocks without making a big deal of it, and it keeps my week manageable.
Before our next meet, I message the teacher about the deadline step by step, while my battery still holds.
When my teacher emails back, I rewrite three lines of my essay just to be safe, so nothing piles up later.
Right after school, I fix the code example in our report step by step, without skipping dinner.
During study hall, I compare answers with Maya just in case, so nothing piles up later.
Once I grab a snack, I clean up the shared doc just in case, so nothing piles up later.
During lunch, I ask my friend to check my outline without making a big deal of it, since the deadline quietly shifted.
Before our next meet, I rewrite the opening paragraph without making a big deal of it, so I am not scrambling at night.
If we finish early, I make a small checklist for tomorrow just to be safe, so nothing piles up later.
Since our project got moved, I ask my sister to quiz me just in case, while my phone stays on silent.
During study hall, I finally update my study sheet just in case, since we present on Friday.
If we finish early, I fix the code example in our report as a quick reset, without skipping dinner.
Before homeroom, I review my notes for ten minutes as a quick reset, because practice runs late.
If we finish early, I send the slide draft to my partner just to be safe, without skipping dinner.
When the wifi cooperates, I rewrite three lines of my essay as a quick reset, while the hallway is still calm.
During study hall, I send the slide draft to my partner without making a big deal of it, while my phone stays on silent.
Right after school, I clean up the shared doc as a quick reset, so I do not lose track.
When the wifi cooperates, I compare answers with Maya just in case, and I can actually sleep on time.
When the hallway clears out, I review my notes for ten minutes just to be safe, so I do not lose track.
